channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: Private Markets’ Software Pain Is About to Get a Lot Worse
item.description: The reckoning facing private markets has only just begun with a wall of software debt set to mature

item.title: Goldman Sachs Sees Highest Energy Capex Since Shale Revolution
item.description: Michele Della Vigna, head of EMEA natural resources research at Goldman Sachs, discusses the outlook for energy industry investment with the Strait of Hormuz becoming "more risky" and US shale "peaking." Speaking on Bloomberg Television, Della Vigna says the US-Iran conflict has created a new oil-price equilibrium. "The industry has been underinvesting in hydrocarbons for too long," he adds. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Goldman Flags $100-Plus Brent If Hormuz Shut Another Month
item.description: Brent crude is set to average more than $100 a barrel through 2026 if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed for another month, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Emerging-Market Rebound Stalls as Iran Truce Doubts Lift Oil
item.description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies fell for the first time in five days as a shaky start to the Middle East ceasefire again pushed up oil prices.

item.title: Riskier Hybrid Debt Makes Comeback as European Bond Sales Jump
item.description: A tentative ceasefire in the Iran conflict is fueling a rush to issue new bonds in Europe’s primary market, with Thursday on track for the busiest day since early February.

item.title: Wall Street Still Loves Bitcoin Even if Fever Is Cooling
item.description: Morgan Stanley rolls out its first Bitcoin fund.

item.title: Trump Vows To Keep Troops In Place Before Iran Talks | The Opening Trade 4/9/2026
item.description:
US President Donald Trump vowed to keep US troops in the Persian Gulf ahead of talks with Iran that are planned to firm up a fragile truce. Both sides accused each other of violating the truce that was announced Tuesday after six weeks of fighting, with a disagreement over whether the ceasefire extends to Lebanon emerging as a key flashpoint that could unravel the accord.
The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Guy Johnson. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: S&P Cuts Philippines Outlook to Stable on Impact of Iran War
item.description: S&P Global Ratings lowered its outlook on the Philippines to stable from positive, saying the war in the Middle East has raised risks for the country’s balance of payments and fiscal position.

item.title: UAE Oil Boss Says Hormuz Still Shut With Iran Restricting Access
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed with Iran still restricting access and preventing energy flows to global markets, the head of the biggest oil producer in the United Arab Emirates said. Tehran says transits must be under its supervision.

item.title: Reliance Caps Fuel Sales at $11 Per Pump Amid Growing Shortages
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. has capped fuel purchases across its retail outlets — limiting customers to about $11 worth of gasoline or diesel — as the Middle East crisis continues to choke off supplies despite a tentative ceasefire.

item.title: Nvidia Shares Near Level Where Technical Traders See a Breakout
item.description: After months of sluggish returns, Nvidia Corp.’s stock is rallying again and close to breaking out of its narrow trading range, which market technicians see as a bullish signal.

item.title: Equities Reaction to Ceasefire Seen as ‘Pretty Rational’ by HSBC’s Kettner
item.description: Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC, examines the relationship between equities and oil in response to the US-Iran ceasefire and sees a reduction in oil prices to near $80 later this year as “good enough” to drive markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: OECD Urges Reeves to Overhaul UK Tax System to Boost Growth
item.description: The OECD urged UK Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves to overhaul the tax system to make it less complex and help turn around Britain’s tepid growth rates.

item.title: Iran War: US, Iran Prepare for Talks in Pakistan | The Pulse 4/9
item.description:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.
Today's guests: Ludovic Subran, Allianz, CIO & Chief Economist; Anu Talus, European Data Protection Board, Chair; Michele Della Vigna, Goldman Sachs, Head of EMEA Natural Resources Research; Craig Nicol, Sona Asset Management, Head of Credit Strategy. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Stock Rally Stalls, Oil Rebounds Ahead of US-Iran Talks, Ceasefire Doubts | Bloomberg Brief 4/9/2026
item.description: US equity futures fall and oil rises after optimism surrounding the US-Iran ceasefire fade. President Trump pledging to keep US troops in the Persian Gulf ahead of talks between the two sides, which will be led by Vice President JD Vance. Nadia Martin Wiggin of Svelland Capital discusses the moves in oil and Max Kettner of HSBC on how crucial CPI will be when assessing the impact of the war with Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: US Lithium Developer to List on Nasdaq in $573 Million SPAC Deal
item.description: A US lithium developer is preparing to go public on the Nasdaq through a blank-check company in a deal that values the firm at roughly $573 million.

item.title: Venezuela Airport Reawakens in Early Sign of Economic Recovery
item.description: Empty faucets, shuttered storefronts, scant arrivals and departures.

item.title: Wheat Rises Amid Iran Ceasefire Worries and Looming US Crop Data
item.description: Wheat rebounded from a one-month low in Chicago as optimism over the ceasefire in the Middle East faded, with any re-escalation potentially threatening further supply chain disruptions and higher price for farm inputs.

item.title: Thailand Seizes $260 Million Assets Linked to Alleged Scammers
item.description: Thai authorities seized assets worth 8.3 billion baht ($260 million) belonging to people behind an alleged money-laundering network linked to transnational cyber scam operations in Cambodia.

item.title: Gold Advances as Traders Weigh Fragile Ceasefire in Iran War
item.description: Gold inched higher for a third day as traders weighed the prospect of a diplomatic resolution to the Iran war, even as ongoing tensions threatened to derail a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Oil Chaos Is Clearest in the Hunt for ASAP Barrels
item.description: With the market suffering a massive loss of supply, refiners are scouting the world for crude.

item.title: UniCredit Helps Commerzbank’s Polish Unit With SRT Deal
item.description: Commerzbank AG’s subsidiary in Poland is working with UniCredit SpA on a significant risk transfer linked to a portfolio of commercial real estate loans.

item.title: Iran-War Pause Opens Door for Congo’s Maiden Eurobond Sale
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is rushing to sell its maiden dollar bonds, taking advantage of a ceasefire in the war between the US and Iran.

item.title: Philippines Says China Fired Flares in Airspace ‘Bullying’
item.description: The Philippines said Chinese forces fired flares directly at its coast guard aircraft over the Spratly Islands in the disputed South China Sea, calling the incident “a clear and deliberate act of bullying.”

item.title: Earnings Optimism Enhances Stocks’ Allure as Iran Worries Ease
item.description: Investors that slashed their stock exposure as war flared in the Middle East may soon have a fresh reason for piling back into equities: the upcoming earnings season.

item.title: Ceasefire Only Seen as a Headline by Oil Market, Says Analyst Schork
item.description: Stephen Schork, principal and editor at the Schork Group, explains the factors showing why the global oil market is highly skeptical of the US-Iran ceasefire, despite the slide in prices on Wednesday. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CoreWeave, Instacart, Texas Instruments
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.3% as of 7:55 a.m. in New York as optimism about the ceasefire in the Middle East wavers.

item.title: Marsh’s Mercer Raises $3.8 Billion for Private Investments
item.description: Marsh & McLennan Cos’ Mercer has raised just over $3.8 billion for private investments.

item.title: US Stock Futures Slip as Ceasefire Doubts Cool Relief Rally
item.description: US equity futures slipped on Thursday, paring the previous session’s sharp rally as skepticism over the US-Iran ceasefire crept back into markets and geopolitical risks remained elevated.

item.title: Stocks Fall as Ceasefire Doubts Boost Oil Prices: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks fell and oil rose as optimism over the US-Iran ceasefire faded, with both sides accusing each other of violating the deal announced after almost six weeks of fighting.
